verses.text: When Syria and Israel marched against Jerusalem, the house of David trembled like wind-shaken trees. Fear gripped the king. The threat of enemies tests whether a man will trust God or his own dread.
When Syria and Israel marched against Jerusalem, the house of David trembled like wind-shaken trees. Fear gripped the king. The threat of enemies tests whether a man will trust God or his own dread.

God sends Isaiah to tell Ahaz, 'Fear not,' for these two kings are but 'smoking firebrands,' and their plot shall not stand. Their menace is nearly spent. God bids His people not to dread foes He has already doomed.

God sends Isaiah to tell Ahaz, 'Fear not,' for these two kings are but 'smoking firebrands,' and their plot shall not stand. Their menace is nearly spent. God bids His people not to dread foes He has already doomed.
'It shall not stand,' the LORD declares; but 'if ye will not believe, surely ye shall not be established.' Faith is the condition. Stability comes only to those who rest on God's word.
'It shall not stand,' the LORD declares; but 'if ye will not believe, surely ye shall not be established.' Faith is the condition. Stability comes only to those who rest on God's word.
'It shall not stand,' the LORD declares; but 'if ye will not believe, surely ye shall not be established.' Faith is the condition. Stability comes only to those who rest on God's word.

God offers Ahaz any sign he asks, but the faithless king refuses, cloaking unbelief as piety: 'I will not tempt the LORD.' He hides distrust behind religious words. To decline God's offered help is not reverence but rebellion.

God offers Ahaz any sign he asks, but the faithless king refuses, cloaking unbelief as piety: 'I will not tempt the LORD.' He hides distrust behind religious words. To decline God's offered help is not reverence but rebellion.

Wearying God, the house of David is given a sign: 'a virgin shall conceive, and bear a son, and shall call his name Immanuel.' This points to Christ. God With Us, born of a virgin, is the great hope beyond all kings' failures.

Wearying God, the house of David is given a sign: 'a virgin shall conceive, and bear a son, and shall call his name Immanuel.' This points to Christ. God With Us, born of a virgin, is the great hope beyond all kings' failures.

The child shall eat curds and honey, and before he knows good from evil the threatening lands shall be forsaken. Deliverance is near at hand. The very timing of a child's growth measures out God's coming rescue.

The child shall eat curds and honey, and before he knows good from evil the threatening lands shall be forsaken. Deliverance is near at hand. The very timing of a child's growth measures out God's coming rescue.

Yet because of Ahaz's unbelief, God will bring the king of Assyria, who shall shave the land 'with a razor that is hired.' The feared ally becomes the scourge. Trusting Assyria instead of God brings Assyria's ruin.

In that day the land shall be so wasted that a man keeps but a cow and a few sheep, and fields become briers and thorns. Desolation follows. Where men forsake God, the land itself bears the marks of judgment.
